Container.Name: Bitwarden Lite
Container.Overview: Unofficial Unraid template for the official Bitwarden Lite self-hosted password manager image.
Container.Description: This is an unofficial Unraid template and is not supported, endorsed, or maintained by Bitwarden. It runs the official Bitwarden Lite single-container self-hosted image. This template uses SQLite by default for a simple one-container Unraid install. Before starting, get an installation ID and key from https://bitwarden.com/host/ and set BW_DOMAIN to the hostname or IP address users will use to reach this server. SSL is enabled by default; provide certificates in /etc/bitwarden or use the generated self-signed certificate for testing.

Config.Name: HTTPS Web UI Port
Config.Description: Container HTTPS port used by the Unraid WebUI button. Required when BW_ENABLE_SSL is true.

Config.Name: HTTP Port
Config.Description: Container HTTP port. Use only if SSL is disabled or when proxying internally.

Config.Name: Bitwarden Data
Config.Description: Persistent Bitwarden Lite data, generated files, certificates, and the default SQLite vault.db.

Config.Name: Bitwarden Logs
Config.Description: Persistent Bitwarden Lite service logs.

Config.Name: Domain
Config.Description: Hostname or IP address where Bitwarden will be accessed, without a scheme or trailing slash. For local testing, this can be your Unraid server IP.

Config.Name: Installation ID
Config.Description: Installation ID from https://bitwarden.com/host/.

Config.Name: Installation Key
Config.Description: Installation key from https://bitwarden.com/host/.

Config.Name: Database Provider
Config.Description: Database provider. Default sqlite creates vault.db under /etc/bitwarden. Other valid values include mysql, mariadb, postgresql, and sqlserver.

Config.Name: SQLite File
Config.Description: SQLite database file path inside the container. The default stores vault.db in the persistent /etc/bitwarden appdata mount.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: Container user ID. Unraid default is nobody.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Container group ID. Unraid default is users.

Config.Name: Enable IPv6
Config.Description: Enable IPv6 support in the Bitwarden Lite web server.

Config.Name: External DB Server
Config.Description: External database hostname or IP. Required for mysql, mariadb, postgresql, or sqlserver.

Config.Name: External DB Port
Config.Description: Optional custom database port. Leave blank to use the default for the selected database provider.

Config.Name: External DB Name
Config.Description: External database name. Required for mysql, mariadb, postgresql, or sqlserver.

Config.Name: External DB Username
Config.Description: External database username. Required for mysql, mariadb, postgresql, or sqlserver.

Config.Name: External DB Password
Config.Description: External database password. Required for mysql, mariadb, postgresql, or sqlserver.

Config.Name: Enable SSL
Config.Description: Set true for Bitwarden Lite to terminate HTTPS itself. If using a reverse proxy for HTTPS, set false.

Config.Name: SSL Certificate
Config.Description: SSL certificate filename under /etc/bitwarden when BW_ENABLE_SSL is true.

Config.Name: SSL Key
Config.Description: SSL private key filename under /etc/bitwarden when BW_ENABLE_SSL is true.

Config.Name: Enable SSL CA
Config.Description: Use SSL with a certificate authority-backed service.

Config.Name: SSL CA Certificate
Config.Description: SSL CA certificate filename under /etc/bitwarden when BW_ENABLE_SSL_CA is true.

Config.Name: Enable SSL DH
Config.Description: Use SSL with Diffie-Hellman parameters.

Config.Name: SSL DH Parameters
Config.Description: Diffie-Hellman parameters filename under /etc/bitwarden when BW_ENABLE_SSL_DH is true.

Config.Name: SSL Protocols
Config.Description: Optional NGINX SSL protocols override. Leave blank for Bitwarden recommended defaults.

Config.Name: SSL Ciphers
Config.Description: Optional NGINX SSL ciphers override. Leave blank for Bitwarden recommended defaults.

Config.Name: HTTP Port Variable
Config.Description: Internal HTTP listener port used by Bitwarden Lite. Keep in sync with the container port mapping target.

Config.Name: HTTPS Port Variable
Config.Description: Internal HTTPS listener port used by Bitwarden Lite when SSL is enabled. Keep in sync with the container port mapping target.

Config.Name: Enable Admin Service
Config.Description: Enable the admin service. Bitwarden docs say not to disable this service.

Config.Name: Enable API Service
Config.Description: Enable the API service. Bitwarden docs say not to disable this service.

Config.Name: Enable Identity Service
Config.Description: Enable the identity service. Bitwarden docs say not to disable this service.

Config.Name: Enable Icons Service
Config.Description: Enable the website icon service for vault item URIs.

Config.Name: Enable Notifications Service
Config.Description: Enable push notifications, login with device, mobile vault sync notifications, and related notification features.

Config.Name: Enable Events Service
Config.Description: Enable Bitwarden event logs for teams and enterprise event monitoring.

Config.Name: Enable SCIM Service
Config.Description: Enable SCIM for Enterprise organizations.

Config.Name: Enable SSO Service
Config.Description: Enable SSO services for Enterprise organizations.

Config.Name: Proxy Icons To Cloud
Config.Description: Proxy icon requests to Bitwarden cloud to reduce local memory use. Bitwarden recommends setting BW_ENABLE_ICONS=false when this is true.

Config.Name: SMTP Reply-To Email
Config.Description: Reply-to email address used by this Bitwarden server.

Config.Name: SMTP Host
Config.Description: SMTP server hostname.

Config.Name: SMTP Port
Config.Description: SMTP server port.

Config.Name: SMTP SSL
Config.Description: Set true for SMTP over SSL. Set false for TLS.

Config.Name: SMTP Username
Config.Description: SMTP username.

Config.Name: SMTP Password
Config.Description: SMTP password. Bitwarden notes that dollar signs are not supported in SMTP passwords.

Config.Name: Yubico Client ID
Config.Description: Yubico Web Services client ID.

Config.Name: Yubico Secret Key
Config.Description: Yubico Web Services secret key.

Config.Name: Admin Portal Emails
Config.Description: Comma-separated email addresses allowed to access the Bitwarden system administrator portal.

Config.Name: Disable User Registration
Config.Description: Set true after creating your initial account to prevent open public registration.

Config.Name: HIBP API Key
Config.Description: Have I Been Pwned API key for breach reports and master password checks.

Config.Name: Real IPs
Config.Description: Comma-separated trusted proxy IPs for NGINX real client IP handling.

Config.Name: Content Security Policy
Config.Description: Optional Content-Security-Policy override. Changing this can break Bitwarden features.
